author: Signal2Noise
title: Useful
content:
I like this app for music playback of my local library. I would go Premium but the math is strange.

Monthly 0.99 = 11.88 / year
Yearly = 12.99 (so 1.11 more than paying by month)
Lifetime @ 39.99 is a 3 year cost up front so not sure how long FlacBox will be supported in the future. Lots of similar music apps seem to die out sooner than later.

author: Canadian maple syrup guy
title: Clean and simple
content:
This app brings me back to the days of when you purchased something on the store you can enjoy with a peace of mind with no subscription. Worth the life time purchase. It’s a tragedy the default Apple Music app doesn’t support flac in 2024. 

Suggestion feature: it would be cool for the album art to go all the way to the side edges and the action buttons to take on a blurred background(colors) of the album art.

author: Everappz
title: J’ai payé pour ça ?
content:
Je cherchais une façon simple d’écouter mes fichiers audio sur mon cloud. 

Great until I change for iPhone 15. The support at everappz is so not there.

Don’t pay for that.

title: Good update
content: Good update, responsive developer. Thank you!

author: Alex B B
title: Excellent app to replace Spotify
content:
I must be old school, in that I prefer the simplicity of having easy access to my music collection, stored on cloud, and I do not love being bombarded with suggestions for new music.
As such, competing music services, such as YouTube, premium, or Spotify do not work for me.
This app is one stop shop for everything music and audiobook related: store all of my files on my own cloud(not forced to use iCloud), cache whatever I wanna listen to for the next several weeks for off-line use. As of the time of writing, no other iOS app can do this.
Lastly, the developers are active constantly making improvements , fixing bugs and API interfaces with many cloud services. This is a lot of work, and the developer should be applauded for keeping it up!

author: Dadalalune
title: Bug
content:
Impossible de l’ouvrir. Dès que je demande de faire une plage, il saute.
Une solution?
Merci

author: phantomic109
title: New Bug
content: App has mostly been working really well for me. But today I spent hours downloading and setting up new music, and then closed the app, and when I went back in, all of my new music and even some of my previous music would not play at all. It worked perfectly fine playing it from the Files app but for some reason it just isn’t working in this app right now. Hopefully that’ll change on its own, but if nothing works in the next few days I might have to try using a different app.

author: Moosestock
title: UI Could be Better
content:
The app will randomly jump to a seemingly random spot in a song when ending one song and starting the next. 
I put my entire library of songs on random, which should have given me tens of hours of continuous music, and only got two to three hours of music before it stopped. 
These are small things but it’s the small things you have to get right in order to be number one. 
Note: I wrote this review just after the app stopped playing my entire library after only a few hours. I was not impressed.

author: GreatSouls
title: It has some issues…
content: I paid for the premium version. The wifi connection when syncing files is unstable. I have no issue syncing music with other apps, I am on 5G wifi and the signal is full bars. Using wi-fi drive works fine for uploading music, however using the offline feature would be nice if it works…

author: Frsh_35
title: Little annoying bugs
content: Great app overall, but sometimes there are issues; when you open the app and select a song it takes a very long time to load; also when you try to select a specific time of a song it skips to the next track. I don’t know if these issues exist only in m the free version tho or maybe it just meeds a software update. Besides that it is a very useful app.

author: lolilolalolou
title: Doublons et instable
content: Application instable et problème de doublons. Sur environ 130 chansons que j’ai mi dans l’application, j’en ai maintenant plus de 16000. Toutes des doublons des 130 chansons. Donc difficile de passer d’une chanson à une autre. Fonctionne aléatoirement avec CarPlay. Souvent il faut ouvrir l’application directement du smartphone pour que ça fonctionne ensuite avec CarPlay. C’est un peu dangereux quand on conduit. Difficile aussi de mettre des chansons dans l’application du premier coup. Il faut souvent s’y reprendre plusieurs fois. Déjà signalé les problèmes. Jamais eu de réponse… ni de mise à jour (qui sont très rare) pouvant corriger les problèmes.

author: Lyndsay🌷
title: the only app with a one-time payment for premium
content:
i absolutely hate subscription-based services that gatekeep the best features behind a monthly subscription. 

premium for this app is basically a one-time payment to get rid of ads and have more playlists. i respect it.

author: JeffyMac2022
title: Great app - exactly what I wanted.
content: Great app - exactly what I wanted.  I digitized all of my music collection and stored it on a NAS. Wanted a non-Apple way to play this music without having to deal with iTunes or iMusic or iWhatever. Flacbox fit the bill perfectly. Great playback quality. Intuitive interface. Easy to navigate. A+

author: FrsotyCones
title: One of my favourite apps
content: Absolutely worth the purchase!

author: Halfmoon2
title: Nice Interface - NON existing support and I keep having to reload songs
content:
The app has a lot of nice features but I have had to reload my songs 5 times because when I open the app there are zero songs. Each time it takes me approx 7 hours to upload again which seems a long time to load.

Contacted support and so far no reply.

Maybe it does not have issues for people using cloud or network drives.

Bought the app because MusicStreamer which I have used for 5 years does not allow loading from USB hardrive.

Setup is IPad Pro and 2 TB passport hard drive. 

Would like to see the app automatically switch to the sampling rate of the music being played.

author: ktraxx89
title: A BEAUTIFUL workaround for listening to your own creations
content:
In a world of endless subscriptions/cloud services and DRM music streaming apps, somehow the simple functions of a personal MP3 player don’t seem possible anymore - until I found this app…

I love to mix my own beats as a hobby. To listen to the mixes on the go after all that hard work (best part) I used to use Apple Music to upload my music to my iCloud library (don’t ask me how) but for whatever reason this little trick stopped working on me. That’s when I discovered this little gem. So simple to use. Just create a DropBox account, plop in the ridiculously massive WAV’s, FLAC’s, AIF’s or whatever you fancy, open this app and bang - instantly available on the go. LOVVE how you can download to your phone with this too. No more blitzing through my data listening to massive 500+ mB WAV files on repeat. Massive functionality for a free app. Thank you for putting this out there 🙏🙏🙏

author: HaveYouBeen ToldToday
title: Good sound quality!!!
content: The sound quality is very good compared to some other apps I’ve used in the past. The only thing really was it was a little confusing to get going and use at first. Additionally being able to access the apple iTunes music already installed by your computer instead of copying the entire library again and eating storage. Apple phones don’t use an SD card, remember.? Just sayin’ lol. Thanks good app!

author: Skahteee
title: Good so far
content: Impressive that it synchronized my library of 2500+ albums. However, PLEASE add album sort by ARTIST!!!! Browsing a large album collection is much more difficult by title only.

author: Stich0203
title: Great sound but app functionality is very buggy
content: Playing Flac files from the cloud sounds fantastic especially with the EQ. I find the app functionality really annoying. Lots of bugs. If you click on an album, you’ll see the tracks in reverse order and the sort button only sorts in one direction. I sent a message to the developer about this and didn’t hear back. Also, when playing an album from the cloud, it downloads the album and shows as available in offline library. Lots of bugs for the price.

author: Christopher.Day
title: Good but....
content: Works well though there is an annoying bug where if you set Repeat to “One Song” and close the player when you’re finished, it will lose the behaviour even though the repeat icon says it is set to one song.

author: msvadi
title: Track order not preserved
content:
Update 2/21: no gapless playback - it’s a dealbreaker for me

One small issue ruins the app for me: it doesn’t sort tracks properly, especially for multi-disc albums. My ALAC tracks appear in the right order in my iTunes library and have tags for the track number and disc number. But after downloading to the Flacbox, disc numbers are ignored, and everything appears in a random order. I’ll wait with purchasing the pro version until this is fixed.

author: JSMtl201104
title: So many bugs. No response from support
content: Title says it all. Two requests to support have been met with silence. Bugs ranging from sudden inability to connect with network folder, to having all songs with the same (wrong) album cover. Very annoying.

author: Kaivai234
title: Perfect for Linux Users
content:
Simply spin up a samba server, and you can browse/download/stream your music. All of this directly from your phone, without needing to tinker with ifuse libmobiledevice etc.

The interface is very polished - browse by genre, artist, song etc. And supporting flac to boot?! 

Thank you so much for writing this fantastic software, it is a staple on every iOS device I own.
